Patch Set 2:

"However, if a file is created with
O_APPEND and its create is handled by a kernel thread that has
previously created a file with a default list of OSTs, then those
defaults were erroneously applied to the O_APPEND file."

This description sounds like some memory isn't getting cleared that should be cleared?
Uploaded patch set 3.

Do you think that the existing tests are adequate for testing this case, since it failed a small percentage of the time? Or would it make sense to add a new test that specifically targets this case?

Patch Set 3:

> The latest revision takes the approach you suggested of clearing
 > the op_array when it shouldn't be used in the append case.
 > 
 > Do you think that the existing tests are adequate for testing this
 > case, since it failed a small percentage of the time? Or would it
 > make sense to add a new test that specifically targets this case?

Thomas, I've pushed patch https://review.whamcloud.com/51602 to add extra testing for this patch.  I included your test case into the existing subtest and am running it in a loop.  500x is excessive given that each iteration is about 20-30s, so they it take about 4h to finish but still better than crashing/failing random other sessions.

See also https://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/PUB/Changing+Test+Parameters+with+Gerrit+Commit+Messages to see what is possible for this.

There were 3 crashes like this in 24 hours. https://knox.linuxhacker.ru/crashdb_ui_external.py.cgi?newid=68931

Now it turned out this is not entirely new crash and there's a somewhat similar crash that was sporadically observed before this patch (but never coming out of sanity 270):

you can see all prior occurences of it here: https://knox.linuxhacker.ru/crashdb_ui_external.py.cgi?newid=68581

revertign this patch made these all disappear (I only tested for 24 hours), but it does creaet a very notable uptick in sanity test 27M crashes, so it does show this patch is effective against those. We just need to ensure we are not introducing anything new here I think.

Can you please look into it?

parents.subject: LU-17019 tests: fix 'v' usage in test 185

commit.subject: LU-16872 lod: reset llc_ostlist when using O_APPEND stripes
commit.message:
LU-16872 lod: reset llc_ostlist when using O_APPEND stripes

Files created with O_APPEND can have special striping set with the
parameters mdd.*.append_stripe_count and mdd.*.append_pool, and
should not inherit a list of OSTs to use from a parent directory
when these parameters are set. However, if a file is created with
O_APPEND and its create is handled by a kernel thread that has
previously created a file with a default list of OSTs, then those
defaults were erroneously applied to the O_APPEND file. This can
lead to the create returning EINVAL or to a crash.

This commit ensures that llc_ostlist is cleared when a file is
created with special append stripes.

Signed-off-by: Thomas Bertschinger <bertschinger@lanl.gov>
Change-Id: Ib2023e17c9ef31a2e029e09e67b257eb2c77b113
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51559
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
